changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_audit_logging_configured to better reflect the configuration aspect
Renamed k8s_apiserver_audit_log_file_validation_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_log_integrity_validation to clarify the focus on integrity
review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control SI-4 (20) focusing on monitoring privileged user activities. However, slight adjustments in naming improve clarity and adherence to naming conventions. The functions map accurately to Kubernetes security features like audit logging and RBAC monitoring.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'configured' instead of 'enabled' for audit logging to emphasize setup completeness
Clarify 'validation' to 'integrity_validation' for audit log file checks to specify the security aspect
title: System Monitoring | Privileged Users
step1_reasoning: The control SI-4 (20) focuses on monitoring privileged user activities. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that audit logging is enabled and configured to capture actions by privileged users. Additionally, RBAC can be used to identify and monitor privileged roles. The API server audit logs should be validated to ensure integrity, similar to cloud log file validation.
